notes: Advisor benchmark scenarios are answer-quality checks; they should not require disk changes.
Run benchmarks only after adding a judge/verifier that can score advisory quality for the scenario.
Do not run these scenarios against Claude Code; Advisor is a Codex-only plugin.
setupQuestions: Which planning decisions should Advisor materially improve for this repo?
Which scenario is the must-pass Advisor delegation case?
What rubric distinguishes useful pushback from generic planning advice?
Which boundary prompt should Advisor skip or narrow instead of overreaching?

scenarios.title: Recommend a path through fuzzy tradeoffs
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether Advisor turns an ambiguous engineering/product choice into a recommended path with scoped tradeoffs.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "advisor" if it helps. We are deciding whether to add provider-scoped behavior evals to a multi-harness plugin marketplace. Recommend the path, call out decisions the user must make, cut unnecessary scope, and name risks to verify. Do not edit files.
successChecklist: The response recommends one path instead of presenting a neutral menu.
The response identifies hard-to-reverse decisions and reasonable defaults.
The response cuts or defers at least one nonessential scope item.
The response stays read-only and does not claim to edit files.

scenarios.title: Shape a ticket plan without creating tickets
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether Advisor produces an implementation-ready ticket outline while preserving the approval gate for real ticket creation.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "advisor" if it helps. Turn this fuzzy request into a ticket plan proposal: migrate a local planning helper into a Codex-only marketplace plugin, keep it out of Claude Code, and preserve validation. Do not create real tickets or edit files.
successChecklist: The response proposes ordered tickets with observable value.
The response separates prerequisites, in-scope work, non-goals, and risks.
The response explicitly avoids creating real tickets without approval.
The response keeps Claude Code exclusion as a requirement.

scenarios.title: Skip or narrow Advisor for scoped implementation
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether Advisor avoids over-triggering on narrow implementation tasks.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "advisor" if it helps. Fix a typo in README.md and run formatting. Explain whether Advisor is appropriate for this request. Do not edit files.
successChecklist: The response says Advisor is not needed or should be skipped for the narrow task.
The response does not run a broad planning loop for a typo fix.
The response gives the direct implementation and verification path.
The response stays read-only and does not claim to edit files.
